channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Louis McCartney will lead 'Stranger Things: The First Shadow' on Broadway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Something wicked this way comes: Louis McCartney who starred in the play “Stranger Things: The First Shadow” in London will reprise his creepy monster on Broadway.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harris says Trump 'is a fascist' after his ex-chief of staff says he wanted generals like Hitler's
item.description: ASTON, Pa. (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris said that she believes that Donald Trump "is a fascist” after his longest-serving chief of staff said the former president praised Adolf Hitler while in office and put personal loyalty above the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: DAN MERICA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Evangelicals emerge as a potent lobby for US support of Ukraine
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Since the war in Ukraine began, Yaroslav Pyzh, a Baptist pastor in Lviv, has worn many hats. He runs the large and growing Ukrainian Baptist Theological Seminary, while overseeing a network of 18 humanitarian aid centers across the embattled country.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Funeral home owners accused of storing decaying bodies expected to plead guilty to COVID-19 fraud
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Colorado funeral home owners accused of misspending nearly $900,000 in COVID-19 pandemic relief funds and living lavishly, all while allegedly stashing 190 decaying bodies in a building and sending grieving families fake ashes, are expected to plead guilty to federal charges Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia men who killed Ahmaud Arbery want a new trial
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — Three white men serving life in prison for murder after chasing and killing Ahmaud Arbery in 2020 are returning to court Thursday to ask a judge for a new trial.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One in five neighbors have fought over politics: Survey
item.description: Among those surveyed, men were more than twice as likely to get into a dispute than women, according to Redfin.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia, candidates target of ‘sustained’ cyberattacks: Reports
item.description: CNN says Georgia fended off the likely overseas attack, while Microsoft says China, Russia and Iran are targeting the U.S. election process.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Man wrongly convicted of murder starts new job after 28 years behind bars
item.description: “I knew they have a good reputation of training individuals getting ready for the workforce,” Jones said. He had two sons who completed the program.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Bernthal, NewsNation

item.title: Quarter Pounders are off the menu at 20% of McDonald's in US as E. coli cases are investigated
item.description: McDonald’s worked Wednesday to reassure customers that its U.S. restaurants are safe as federal investigators tried to pinpoint the cause of a deadly E. coli outbreak linked to the fast-food giant's Quarter Pounder hamburgers. McDonald's pulled Quarter Pounders from one-fifth of its U.S.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A man who threatened to kill Democratic election officials pleads guilty
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A Colorado man repeatedly made online threats about killing the top elections officials in his state and Arizona — both Democrats — as well as a judge and law enforcement agents, according to a guilty plea he entered Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Laredo lifts boil water order after E. coli 'crisis' infiltrated city's water
item.description: he South Texas border city of Laredo has lifted its boil-water order after dangerous E. coli was found in the city's water system, which has been blamed on illegal or faulty water connections.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Political strategist James Carville lists 3 reasons why he thinks Harris will win
item.description: “The biggest reason Mr. Trump will lose is that the whole Republican Party has been on a losing streak since Mr. Trump took it over,” James Carville said in an essay published by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Harris pitches herself as 'pragmatic capitalist' in push for Latino voters
item.description: Vice President Harris is pitching herself as a “pragmatic capitalist” in a Noticias Telemundo interview as she looks to court Latino voters less than two weeks out from the election. “I am a capitalist.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Goldfish changes name of iconic crackers to appeal to adults
item.description: Snack lovers can look forward to a playful twist on a childhood favorite, as Goldfish announces a limited-time renaming of its iconic Cheddar crackers.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, KRON4

item.title: Suspected dog-fighting ring busted; shelter director bitten
item.description: "We are struggling right now each of us individually and as a crew. The challenges are insurmountable," said the animal shelter in a social media post.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: Fallout from 2023 Hamas attack lingers as Jews worldwide prepare for a holiday meant for joy
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Customarily, Simchat Torah is one of the most joyous days of the Jewish calendar, highlighted by exuberant dancing around a Torah. Jewish leaders say the joy will return during this week's celebrations, even as Jews recall that last year's Simchat Torah overlapped with Oct.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-Abercrombie & Fitch CEO grins with ankle monitor after posting $10M bail in sex trafficking case: photos
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch boss Mike Jeffries, accused of sex trafficking and forced prostitution, left Florida court after posting $10 million bail with ankle monitor, new photos show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 arrested after man claims kidnappers amputated his finger in Denver
item.description: Denver police arrested two people in connection to an alleged kidnapping and assault wherein the victim's finger was amputated by his captors.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, ABC 4

item.title: Over 7,100 bottles of prescription antidepressants recalled over potentially cancer-causing substance
item.description: A pharmaceutical company issued a recall this month for thousands of bottles of duloxetine, a medicine often prescribed to treat depression.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: Yelp turns off comments on the McDonald's that Trump worked at after fake reviews
item.description: Yelp recently paused reviews on a McDonald's in Pennsylvania after former President Trump served pre-screened customers at the location Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4

item.title: Judges reject GOP overseas ballot challenges in Michigan, North Carolina
item.description: State judges in Michigan and North Carolina on Monday rejected the Republican National Committee’s (RNC) separate challenges to certain overseas ballots in the key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New campus protest rules spur an outcry from college faculty
item.description: Dissent is thriving this fall at American colleges, and not just among student activists. With student protests limited by new restrictions, faculty have taken up the cause. To faculty, new protest rules threaten freedom of speech — and the freedom to think, both central to university life.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disgraced Hollywood producer Harvey Weinstein diagnosed with bone cancer: Reports
item.description: Disgraced movie producer Harvey Weinstein has been diagnosed with bone cancer, according to reports. The infamous Hollywood mogul’s diagnosis of chronic myeloid leukemia was first reported by NBC News on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: WATCH: Footage appears to show moment helicopter crash topples radio tower in Houston
item.description: A surveillance camera mounted outside of a Houston photo studio appears to have captured the moment a helicopter crash toppled a radio tower on Sunday, killing four people.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks to release 'Wicked' drinks inspired by the upcoming film
item.description: Starbucks is releasing two limited-time drinks inspired by the two main characters in the upcoming film, "Wicked.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Some Jewish voters in presidential swing states reconsider their longtime devotion to Democrats
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — For Rona Kaufman, the signs are everywhere that more Jews feel abandoned by the Democratic Party and may vote for Republican Donald Trump. It's in her Facebook feed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart and Aldi are once again offering meal deals for Thanksgiving
item.description: The two companies are advertising their dinners at "inflation-free" or "pre-inflation" prices, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Who is Lucifer 'Justin Case' Everylove? Meet the presidential candidate who is only on Utah's ballots
item.description: Who is Lucifer "Justin Case" Everylove? Here's how this unaffiliated presidential candidate made it onto the ballots in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5

item.title: What is pink cocaine? Autopsy finds drug in Liam Payne's body
item.description: A partial autopsy conducted on former One Direction singer Liam Payne found multiple substances in his system when he fell to his death from the third-floor balcony of a hotel room in Argentina last week, according to a new report from ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees and Dodgers meet in World Series for 12th time in matchup of Broadway and Hollywood
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Broadway vs. Hollywood. Subway vs. Freeway. Judge vs. Ohtani. New York neighbors who became cross-country rivals, the Yankees and Dodgers renew their starry struggle in the World Series for the first time in 43 years.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Ohio officer stands trial in the 2020 shooting death of Andre Hill
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A white former Ohio police officer has gone on trial Monday in the killing of Andre Hill, a Black man who was shot as he emerged from a garage holding a cellphone nearly four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to consumer safety agency’s structure
item.description: The Supreme Court announced Monday it won’t take up a case that could have delivered another blow to federal agencies. Two conservative groups sought to challenge the structure of the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), which oversees making sure the country’s consumer products are safe.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: How the 2025 Social Security COLA compares to recent years
item.description: A recent survey from the Motley Fool shows 50% of retirees have considered going back to work to make ends meet.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures challenge interpreters
item.description: Vague or technical terms can be challenging, even more so when it comes to Indigenous languages that have only limited written dictionaries.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
